name: {{PACK_NAME}}
_comment_category: QUANTITATIVE packs are the third TMOS13 category alongside CRM (session intelligence) and SIMULATOR (strategic simulation). Quantitative packs are interactive analytical engines — they compute, explain, stress-test, and recommend. The AI is an analyst, not a persona. Every number shows its work. Every assumption is explicit and adjustable.
_comment_architecture: CRM = conversation → structured record → business action. SIMULATOR = situation → persona roleplay → strategic insight. QUANTITATIVE = inputs → interactive model → investment decision support. No roleplay, no persona engine, no hidden state. Instead: calculation engine, assumption tracking, sensitivity analysis, scenario comparison, and data feed integration.

cartridges._comment: REQUIRED: Every quantitative pack needs an intake cartridge that captures the investment parameters. Unlike simulator briefing (which builds a world) or CRM triage (which routes), this builds a MODEL — capturing the numbers, assumptions, and constraints that drive the analysis.
cartridges.name: {{INTAKE_NAME}}
cartridges.description: Describe the investment opportunity. Capture parameters, assumptions, and constraints.

cartridges._comment: REQUIRED: The core analysis cartridge. This is where the math lives. Runs calculations, shows work, explains what each number means, and surfaces the key metrics that drive the decision.
cartridges.name: {{ANALYSIS_NAME}}
cartridges.description: Core financial analysis — calculations, metrics, comparisons.

cartridges._comment: REQUIRED: Stress testing. What if rates change? What if vacancy doubles? What if growth slows? Every quantitative pack needs a scenario engine that varies assumptions and shows how outcomes change.
cartridges.name: Scenario Analysis
cartridges.description: Stress-test assumptions. Compare bull/base/bear cases. Sensitivity analysis on key variables.

cartridges._comment: REQUIRED: The output. Synthesize the analysis into a recommendation with explicit confidence, key risks, decision criteria, and a downloadable report.
cartridges.name: Investment Recommendation
cartridges.description: Synthesized analysis — recommendation, confidence level, key risks, decision criteria.

_comment_optional_cartridges: Domain-specific packs add cartridges between analysis and scenarios. Examples: real_estate adds 'renovation' and 'financing'. stocks adds 'valuation' and 'technical'. Each domain has its own analytical modules.

navigation._comment: Domain-specific navigation patterns go here. Route to analytical cartridges based on what the user is asking about.

features._comment_features: Quantitative packs have NO persona_engine, NO dual_state, NO branching_tree — these are simulator features. Instead they have interactive_calculator (real-time computation), assumption_tracking (every number traced to its source), sensitivity_analysis (vary inputs, see output changes), scenario_comparison (side-by-side bull/base/bear), and data_feeds (MCP integration for real market data).

privacy._comment: Financial analysis sessions contain sensitive investment data. Session-only retention by default. User can opt to save models for future reference.

data_source._comment: When MCP feeds are available, 'hybrid' uses live data for market rates, comps, etc. while accepting user overrides. 'user_input' is fully manual. 'mcp_feed' pulls everything available automatically.

investment._comment: EXTEND with domain-specific investment parameters. This is the core data model — the numbers that drive everything.

assumptions._comment: CRITICAL: Every assumption must be tracked explicitly. The assumption register is the spine of the quantitative pack — it's what makes the analysis auditable and adjustable. Each assumption has a value, a source (user input, market data, estimate), and a confidence level.

model._comment: EXTEND with domain-specific calculated outputs. This is where computed values live — derived from investment parameters + assumptions. Recalculates when any input changes.

decision_tree._comment: Not branching roleplay — but analytical decision points. 'Should I finance with fixed or variable?' 'Should I renovate now or hold?' These are computational branches with different outcome projections.

library.description: Base template for quantitative analysis packs. Fork this to create calculation-heavy experiences with transparent assumptions and sensitivity analysis.
library.author: TMOS13
